MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Of Wismer And Becker Contracting Engineers
Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant and moving party Wismer & Becker Contracting Engineers.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Lisa Moan as Successor-In-Interest to and as wrongful death heir of John Murphy, Deceased.

RULING: DEFENDANT WISMER & BECKER CONTRACTING ENGINEERS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Court declared campaign contributions for the election are posted outside Dept. 503.
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Wismer & Becker Contracting Engineers' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its burden via the factually devoid prong under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. However, plaintiffs created a triable issue of fact as to whether Wismer employees exposed decedent to asbestos dust based on Hanson's testimony that he worked with decedent at Aerojet and that Wismer employees removed insulation and swept up the mess in close proximity to his crew. (Plaintiff's Ex. B (Hanson's July 5, 2018 Deposition) at 26-29, 33-37, 39, 43-44, 58-59.) The Court declines to consider Hanson's July 15, 2011 deposition submitted by defendant as additional evidence in its reply papers. (Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 306.) Defendant's objections are overruled as to Hanson's July 5, 2018 deposition testimony (Plaintiff's Ex. B). Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Diana L. Gonzalez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #7935, dgonzalez@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

RULING: PLAINTIFF BARBARA MURPHY, LISA MOAN, SUSAN ALLEN NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITIONS OF SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIAS PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND CUSTODIAN(S) OF RECORDS AND THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND THE DEPOSITION OF OFFICER-DIRECTOR RAYMOND P. TELLINI OF SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA AND THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to compel the depositions of Scott Co. of California's person(s) most knowledgeable and custodian(s) of records and the production of documents; and the deposition of officer/director Raymond P. Tellini of Scott Co. of California and the production of documents is granted. Scott Co. of California must produce a person(s) most knowledgeable and a custodian(s) of records for deposition and must produce responsive documents according to the December 21, 2016 deposition notice. Additionally, Scott Co. of California must produce Raymond P. Tellini for deposition, along with responsive documents, according to the October 4, 2017 deposition notice. (C.C.P. 2017.210, 2025.230, 2025.280(a).) That defendant provided information about insurance policies in response to special interrogatories does not preclude plaintiffs from noticing these depositions and requesting production of documents. (See C.C.P. 2017.210; Irvington-Moore, Inc. v. Superior Court (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 733.) The Court does not find good cause to restrict such discovery pursuant to C.C.P. 2019.030(a), nor did defendant move for a protective order requesting such relief. (C.C.P. 2019.030(b).)
